text: CEASEFIRE STATUS: Trump declares ceasefire on 'massive life support' after rejecting Iran's counter-proposal as a 'piece of garbage.' Some Trump aides now more seriously considering resumption of major combat operations than at any point since April 8 ceasefire. Trump met with senior military advisers Monday. Sanctions on 12 entities facilitating Iranian oil sales to China announced same day.
verificationNote: CNN, Washington Post, Bloomberg confirmed Trump remarks. Bloomberg confirmed sanctions on 12 entities.

text: NEGOTIATIONS BREAKDOWN: Iran's counter-proposal demanded war reparations, full Hormuz sovereignty, end to naval blockade, sanctions relief, and deferred nuclear talks. US demands Iran surrender ~440 kg highly enriched uranium and accept 10-20yr enrichment moratorium. Gap enormous: Iran offered 5yr moratorium; US demanded 20. Iran proposed uranium transfer to third country with return clause if US exits deal.
verificationNote: CNBC, NBC News, Al Jazeera confirmed details of both proposals.

text: MILITARY/BUDGET: Defense Secretary Hegseth testifies before Senate Armed Services Committee today (Day 2) on $1.5T defense budget. Sen. Mark Kelly pressed Hegseth on munitions depletion — Hegseth admitted restocking would take 'months and years.' Hegseth called Democratic critics 'defeatists from the cheap seats.' Houthi drone intercepted near Eilat — first attack since ceasefire. Israel bombs Lebanon (24 killed May 9).
verificationNote: PBS News, Washington Post confirmed Hegseth testimony. Times of Israel confirmed Eilat drone intercept May 12.

text: TRUMP-XI SUMMIT: Trump departs for Beijing to meet Xi Jinping — Iran war the dominant agenda item. US expects China to pressure Iran. Beijing publicly supports peace but has privately sent 'subtle message of discontent' to Iran for Hormuz closure and US for shipping blockade. US sanctioned 4 China-based firms for providing satellite imagery enabling Iranian strikes on US forces. China told domestic firms to ignore US sanctions on refiners.
verificationNote: CNBC, Washington Times, Bloomberg confirmed Trump-Xi summit agenda. Bloomberg confirmed China satellite imagery sanctions.

text: ISW ASSESSMENT (May 8): IRGC Cmdr Vahidi continues to drive Iran's war and negotiating posture. US officials assess Iranian decision-making remains fragmented. Iran's primary positive strategic objective: gain recognition of sovereignty over Strait of Hormuz, which would reshape global maritime norms. Secondary objective: economic relief to fund ballistic missile reconstitution. Both US and Iranian military officials now describe renewed combat as 'likely.'
verificationNote: Based on ISW/Critical Threats Iran Update Special Report May 8, 2026. Published at criticalthreats.org.

text: POLITICAL: Netanyahu in first US broadcast since war began (60 Minutes, May 10) says war 'not over' until enriched uranium 'taken out' of Iran and enrichment sites dismantled. Says regime change 'possible, not guaranteed' — collapse of regime would end Hezbollah, Hamas, Houthis 'scaffolding.' Iran's Pezeshkian confirmed he met Mojtaba Khamenei in person for 2.5 hours — first official confirmation of new Supreme Leader's whereabouts.
verificationNote: CBS News confirmed Netanyahu 60 Minutes transcript. Euronews confirmed Pezeshkian-Khamenei meeting.

text: HAARETZ ANALYSIS: Iran's resilience is dragging Israel into a war of attrition on the Lebanese front. Israel lacks 'a clear picture of victory' despite military superiority. IDF killed 24 in Lebanon on May 9. Houthi drone intercepted near Eilat May 12 — first attack since ceasefire. Qatar accuses Iran of 'weaponizing' Hormuz and 'blackmailing' Gulf states. Shipping traffic remains at standstill. Oil up 3% after Trump rejected Iran proposal.
verificationNote: Haaretz analysis confirmed. Times of Israel confirmed Eilat interception. CNBC confirmed oil rise.
